title: Air Conditioners - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search 22 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ADVANCED HEALTH HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS CLIMATE SUPPORTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Climate Support is a health-related social need (HRSN) service and is provided by the Oregon Health Plan. HRSN Climate Supports are devices to help keep people safe during climate events. Covered devices are only for use in the member's home or primary residence and may include: -Air conditioners, heaters, and air filters to provide clean air and healthy temperatures -Mini fridges to keep medication cold during a power outage -Portable power supplies to operate medical devices like ventilators during a power outage Members can only receive HRSN climate services once per lifetime. Eligibility: Advanced Health members who meet all of the following: -Have a living situation that qualifies them for HRSN services -Have a health condition that makes climate events challenging or dangerous -Live in a place with electricity -Can use the device safely Air conditioner or Air Filter: -Must not already have an Air Conditioner or Air Filter -Received medical assistance now or have in the past 12 months through OHA, Oregon Department of Human Services (ODHS), or Medicare -Be at risk for heat-related illness because the applicant is: 65 years or older, or a medically fragile child, or has a medical condition that's made worse by heat or smoke Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 269-7400 Visit Website More Details ADVANCED HEALTH FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 883-2947 Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND COOLING PORTLAND PCEF-FUNDED CLIMATE RESILIENCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 311 helps community members who are vulnerable to extreme heat apply for portable heat pumps and cooling units. Applicant information is provided to participating agencies who do the delivery and installation of units, including support for maintaining product life, efficiency, and usability. There may be a waitlist to receive a unit. Community distribution partners are responsible for identifying and selecting recipients in the program. The City of Portland does not select recipients. 1120 SW 5th Avenue Suite 114 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Household must make 60% or less of Area Median Income (AMI), adjusted for household size. Priority for people who meet two of the following factors: -Over 60 years of age -Have medical conditions that increase risk of heat-related illness -Live alone Hours: 7 days per week 7am-8pm; closed on federal holidays Email 311 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA PACIFIC CCO HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS CLIMATE SUPPORTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Climate Support is a health-related social need (HRSN) service and is provided by the Oregon Health Plan. Limited to one unit per household. Households are responsible for installing the units themselves. 17933 NW Evergreen Place Suite 315 Portland, OR 97229 Eligibility: -Age 62 and older -Household income is at or below 60% of Oregon's Area Median Income (AMI) over the past 30 days -Have not received an AC from Community Action in the past 2 years Hours: -Apply by Monday, August 31, 2025 -Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm, closed national holidays Email (503) 615-0771 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION WASHINGTON COUNTY HILLSBORO MULTI-SERVICE CENTER SENIOR PORTABLE AC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers portable air conditioning units. Limited to one unit per household. Households are responsible for installing the units themselves. 11515 SW Durham Road Suite E8 Tigard, OR 97224 Eligibility: -Age 62 and older -Household income is at or below 60% of Oregon's Area Median Income (AMI) over the past 30 days -Have not received an AC from Community Action in the past 2 years Hours: -Apply by Monday, August 31, 2025 -Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm, closed national holidays Email (503) 615-0771 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS CLIMATE SUPPORTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Climate Support is a health-related social need (HRSN) service and is provided by the Oregon Health Plan. HRSN Climate Supports are devices to help keep people safe during climate events. Covered devices are only for use in the member's home or primary residence and may include: -Air conditioners, heaters, and air filters to provide clean air and healthy temperatures -Mini fridges to keep medication cold during a power outage -Portable power supplies to operate medical devices like ventilators during a power outage Members can only receive HRSN climate services once per lifetime. Eligibility: Eastern Oregon CCO members who meeting all of the following: -Have a living situation that qualifies them for HRSN services -Have a health condition that makes climate events challenging or dangerous -Live in a place with electricity -Can use the device safely Air conditioner or Air Filter: -Must not already have an Air Conditioner or Air Filter -Received medical assistance now or have in the past 12 months through OHA, Oregon Department of Human Services (ODHS), or Medicare -Be at risk for heat-related illness because the applicant is: 65 years or older, or a medically fragile child, or has a medical condition that's made worse by heat or smoke Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (503) 765-3521 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (for individuals being discharged from the hospital or who have a set move-in date for a new home and need a temporary place to stay before then) -Electronics such as cell phones, computers, and other communication devices when there are no state programs available in the area -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships or home equipment -Beds and mattresses for children under 21 or for adults with a diagnosis of a back or spine condition -Car seats if there are no other county or programs available in the area All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Visit website to review additional eligibility criteria regarding specific programs and benefits. Covered devices are only for use in the member's home or primary residence and may include: -Air conditioners, heaters, and air filters to provide clean air and healthy temperatures -Mini fridges to keep medication cold during a power outage -Portable power supplies to operate medical devices like ventilators during a power outage Members can only receive HRSN climate services once per lifetime. Eligibility: Members who belong to HealthShare/Providence CCO. Members must meet all of the following: -Have a living situation that qualifies them for HRSN services -Have a health condition that makes climate events challenging or dangerous -Live in a place with electricity -Can use the device safely Air conditioner or Air Filter: -Must not already have an Air Conditioner or Air Filter -Received medical assistance now or have in the past 12 months through OHA, Oregon Department of Human Services (ODHS), or Medicare -Be at risk for heat-related illness because the applicant is: 65 years or older, or a medically fragile child, or has a medical condition that's made worse by heat or smoke Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:45pm Email (503) 574-7247 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INTERCOMMUNITY HEALTH NETWORK CCO HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS CLIMATE SUPPORTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Climate Support is a health-related social need (HRSN) service and is provided by the Oregon Health Plan. HRSN Climate Supports are devices to help keep people safe during climate events. Covered devices are only for use in the member's home or primary residence and may include: -Air conditioners, heaters, and air filters to provide clean air and healthy temperatures -Mini fridges to keep medication cold during a power outage -Portable power supplies to operate medical devices like ventilators during a power outage Members can only receive HRSN climate services once per lifetime. Eligibility: IHN CCO members who meet all of the following: -Have a living situation that qualifies them for HRSN services -Have a health condition that makes climate events challenging or dangerous -Live in a place with electricity -Can use the device safely Air conditioner or Air Filter: -Must not already have an Air Conditioner or Air Filter -Received medical assistance now or have in the past 12 months through OHA, Oregon Department of Human Services (ODHS), or Medicare -Be at risk for heat-related illness because the applicant is: 65 years or older, or a medically fragile child, or has a medical condition that's made worse by heat or smoke Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INTERCOMMUNITY HEALTH NETWORK CCO FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify.
